[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network - Engineering Status Update Graphic**

We’re introducing a new Engineering Status Update graphic to give the Unity community a clear, at-a-glance snapshot of what our engineering team is working on at any given time.

This update is not a complete list of everything in development. Instead, it highlights the key issues the community is seeing and reporting, and shows where those items sit in our engineering workflow.

Here’s how to read it:

Known Issues:
These are issues that the community may be experiencing. If something appears here, it means the team is aware of it and has formally acknowledged it.

In Progress:
These items are actively being worked on by the engineering team.

Completed:
These are issues or tasks that have been resolved. In some cases, we’ll continue to monitor them to ensure long-term stability.

Each update reflects the current build version, so you can clearly see what improvements are tied to each release.

As always, 🙏 thank you for your continued feedback and support as Unity continues to evolve.

msg:
[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network - CLI Verification Task Now Live**

Unity Network is now ready to accommodate international and domestic Caller ID verification testing.

**What is CLI Testing?**
Caller ID (CLI) verification confirms that inbound calls display the correct and expected caller information as they pass through carrier routes into your device.

This is real-world telecommunications verification - validating route integrity across retail and wholesale voice paths.
**
How it works:**
• Opt-in to the CLI task from the Task Tab in the Unity App
• Receive an inbound test call
• You can ignore the call or answer - if answered, the call will disconnect immediately
• Missed the call? That's ok - submit the number you saw on the inbound call at any time within 24 hours

**Network Incentives:**
0.05 to 2.00 UP per call, depending on test parameters.

**What's ahead:**
CLI verification volume on the network is expected to increase as commercial demand scales. SMS CLI verification is coming soon, amongst additional native & opt-in tasks. Opt-in now via the Unity App to start verifying CLIs today.

msg:
[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network - CLI Mini Guide** 

A  quick guide to the new CLI opt-in task.

𝗢𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘃𝗶𝗲𝘄:
CLI tasks require an active Unity license
CLI is an opt-in task, and license operators must enable it
CLI calls are live
Calls are real and non-synthetic
CLI calls look like a regular incoming call
CLI works in every region
Tasks are carrier-agnostic
Any SIM or eSIM that can receive incoming calls is supported
Compatible with iOS, even if the “Unity connection” task shows offline 

𝗡𝗼𝘁𝗶𝗳𝗶𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀:
When a CLI call is received, users get a notification
Notifications include a push notification and an in-app pop-up

𝗠𝗶𝘀𝘀𝗲𝗱 𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗹𝘀**:**
It’s OK if you miss the call
You can report a CLI call up to 24 hours after it’s received

𝗦𝗜𝗠** **𝗯𝗮𝗹𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲 / 𝗮𝗰𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲:
If your SIM or eSIM can receive incoming calls, it can accept CLI tests
You usually do NOT need an active balance on your SIM/eSIM to receive a CLI call, but this depends on your carrier, and some carriers may require it

𝗡𝗲𝘁𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸 𝗜𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲𝘀:
Unity Points are distributed immediately after a valid CLI task is completed 

CLI – verifying telecom activity at scale.

author: Kyle | I will never PM you 1st
msg:
#update

The terms are there to protect against users who are trying to cheat the system. These cheaters know exactly what they are doing.

As long as the ULO does not make use of datacentre IPs, VPS, proxies or automated infrastructure then it is all good.

The team have confirmed that accidental use of VPN's or proxies will not result in a ban - all bans that take place are reviewed manually. In these cases, the team don’t ban you or the ULO, simply the IP is banned. The ULO can continue using their ULO service, on a real IP address / without cheating.

Mistakes are forgiven. The network is not looking to place permanent bans. 

You can also appeal a ban by sending an email to appeals@unitynodes.io

author: Kay | Will never DM you first
msg:
**Is you see "This device is not bound"**

This is not an error, this means that you are trying to message an UNO from ULO device that is not connected to the license. Meaning:
- if you open your phone -> open the license that this device is bound to -> try to message UNO -> you will **NOT** see this message.
- if you open your phone -> open the license that this device is **NOT** bound to -> try to message UNO -> you **WILL** see this message.
(all this provided you're opt-in to the messaging feature)

#update

TLDR by Flippy: That "not bound" message just means it not bound to the actual device you are looking at (in case you have multiple licenses as ULO)

msg:
[File: IMG_1017.MP4] 🤝 **Unity Network - Rewards Update**

During routine network monitoring, we identified operators running thousands of synthetic devices through VPNs, emulators, and /24 IP ranges. These devices were affecting the commercial value of the network. Those accounts have now been addressed, and the network is recalibrating. Please bear with us during this process as reward distribution normalises for genuine operators.

**Why This Matters**
Synthetic activity undermines the commercial value of the entire network. When partners and customers detect synthetic devices, it erodes their confidence in the data and the network itself. Protecting network integrity protects your earning potential.

Please note, there is no limit to the number of devices you may run from a single IP range; however, if a partner or customer determines that your devices are synthetic rather than real-world, it may affect or limit your ability to receive tasks. To maximise reward potential, we recommend running no more than approximately 10 to 20 devices per network subnet, using real telephones, whether headless (for example, those without screens) or fully functional (with screens).

**What's Ahead**
As the network recalibrates, tasks will begin flowing back to legitimate ULOs rather than being consumed by synthetic operators. Your existing telemetry tasks will continue to run as normal. On top of this, we're preparing several new task types for release in the near term, including Scout & Runner (S&R) alongside additional WiFi and SIM powered tasks. Each new task type expands overall reward potential across the network.

Unity Network remains dedicated to delivering a best-in-class edge network experience through the application, with web-based management platforms on the way. There are multiple exciting releases scheduled for March, April, and beyond that have not yet been communicated.

We're also excited to announce that we'll be hosting an AMA with the founder of one of the new third-party projects - a partnership we believe will redefine what rewards mean for ULO operators.

**Questions?**
Submit them to the official channel using #AMA. Your questions will be answered at the UNO meeting on 7 April 2026. Details are available via the X post: https://x.com/UnityNodesIO/status/2036554063936909778

Your feedback matters. We're always working to improve the operator experience and reward structure.

UNOs and ULOs, thank you for your continued support whilst we build out the largest smartphone-driven, multi-versatile, composable edge network.

author: Kay | Will never DM you first
msg:
Reporting low rewards isn’t pointless - it helps the team understand what’s happening on your node. Here’s the thing: not all traffic pays the same. 

A customer buying 100 IPs in the same range (synthetic networking) pays less per IP than a customer buying 1 or 5 unique residential IPs. 

Your node might be serving real traffic but at a lower rate because of the type of task assigned to it. 

That doesn’t mean something is broken - it means demand varies. 

The recalibration and new tooling will help make this clearer, but in the meantime, reporting helps the team build a fuller picture.

Bottom line: different customers pay for different things. If you’re running hundreds of devices on the same IP range, don’t expect the same per-device rewards as someone with a handful of unique residential IPs - the value to the network simply isn’t the same.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
Unity Network additionally supports two distinct developer pathways. 

The first is direct task deployment, where developers launch and run their workloads natively on the Unity Network, leveraging its globally distributed infrastructure spanning 120+ countries and 1,200+ cities. In this model, the developer is a direct participant in the ecosystem - their tasks execute across Unity’s smartphone-powered edge network, benefiting from its reach, redundancy, and decentralised architecture. 

The second pathway is API integration, where developers utilise Unity Network’s capabilities as backend functions within their own platforms, products, or services. Rather than deploying tasks on the network itself, these developers consume Unity’s various distributed, connectivity, or data resources programmatically - embedding them into third-party applications where the end user may never interact with Unity Network directly. Both pathways unlock the power of the same underlying infrastructure, but they serve fundamentally different use cases: one positions Unity Network as the execution environment, the other positions it as an enabling layer beneath the developer’s own stack. 

This is where UNT - the Unity Network Token - becomes central to the economy. Developers who wish to access Unity Network’s powerful, fully functional API to integrate its capabilities into their own projects, platforms, or services pay in UNT. The token acts as the native medium of exchange for API consumption, creating a direct link between network demand and token utility. As more developers build on top of Unity Network’s distributed infrastructure, demand for UNT grows organically - driven not by speculation, but by real usage from real applications tapping into a real network.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
Developers can pay in fiat - we’re not here to create barriers to adoption.

When a developer pays in fiat, we convert it to UNT on their behalf. 

The result is the same: every API call is settled in UNT, every transaction drives demand for the token. 

The difference is we’ve removed the friction. A developer shouldn’t need to navigate token exchanges and wallets just to access an API - but the underlying economy still runs on UNT regardless of how the developer chooses to pay.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg: UNT is not a replacement for tasks - it’s in addition to them. Multiple task types are on the way: WiFi only, SIM only, and hybrid tasks that combine both. These are opportunities you genuinely won’t find elsewhere. The token and the task economy are two separate layers, and operators benefit from both.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
A quick heads up for anyone pulling earnings data from the new portal.

If you’ve noticed it now returns data in small chunks instead of one large response, that’s because the new portal defaults to paginating at 20 records per request. 

The good news is the backend still supports up to 1000 in a single call - you just need to modify the request body.

Default:

{“skip”:0,“take”:20}

Change to:

{“skip”:0,“take”:1000}

This applies to anyone using the JSON data for dashboards, tracking tools, spreadsheets, or anything else that relied on grabbing everything in one go. 

Should get you back to the same workflow you had before with minimal changes on your end.

An advanced and comprehensive public API will be released for developers in an official announcement in the near future.

author: Unity Network Assistant

SMS CLI and Content Testing tackles an industry problem - content swapping and sender ID manipulation. 

Customers need to verify that the SMS content and sender ID arriving at the destination match what was originally sent. 

After you opt in, for this task you’ll see the received SMS and sender ID in your standard SMS inbox just like any normal text message. 

In a future APK version update, the entire process of receiving and reporting the details on the SMS to Unity Network will be automated within the app for a smoother experience.

As for rewards - the potential really depends on several factors including the destination location, origination, volume and the type of content being tested.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
Rewards depend on a number of factors beyond just being a genuine device - network quality, device type, connectivity and the tasks currently running all play a role. 

It’s also worth considering that if those ULOs aren’t directly managed by you, for example if they picked up leases from the marketplace - their actual setup could be anything. 

They could be on low quality networks, running far more devices per network than recommended, they could be synthetic devices or emulators or have other configuration issues that you simply wouldn’t know about. 

The upcoming ULO Network Evaluation Toolkit will change that - giving you full visibility into how each device is graded and what their network setup actually looks like. Combined with the new task types rolling out now and in the coming days, reward potential across a wider range of setups is set to improve.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
Nobody has suggested buying new devices. The evaluation toolkit is designed to give you visibility into how your ULOs are performing - network quality, device grading, connectivity - so you can understand what’s affecting your rewards and make informed decisions. 

That might mean adjusting your network setup, reducing devices per network, or simply understanding which of your ULOs are running in synthetic networks or devices, or which of the ULOs are best suited for which task types. Different tasks value different setups - a device that underperforms for telemetry could be exactly what’s needed for a WiFi-only or SIM-based task. 

It’s about information, not replacement.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

msg:
Tasks being live is not meaningless - it means the infrastructure is built, tested and operational. 

And that’s a massive milestone for any network. Volume doesn’t go from zero to full capacity overnight - it scales as customers onboard, validate the service and increase their usage. 

That process is already happening. CLI testing has active customers who have been testing and are satisfied with the service, and serious contracts are in place that will drive volume up dramatically in the near term.

On top of that, SMS testing went live yesterday, new task types are rolling out in the coming days, and S&R is preparing for public release with reward potential that dwarfs anything currently on the network. 

The volume is building - not every task launches at full throttle on day one, but dismissing what’s live because it hasn’t peaked yet ignores the trajectory this is on.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

msg:
Let’s be clear about a few things. CLI testing and SMS testing operate in segments of the telecoms industry worth billions. These aren’t low value filler tasks. 

When volume scales - and it is scaling - the per-test reward potential is extremely high. A single ULO participating in these tasks stands to earn significantly more than telemetry alone.

The toggle in the app isn’t the finish line - it’s the starting point. The volume is what follows, and it’s coming.

Yesterday alone - multiple new releases went live. SMS testing, web management portals, broadcast messaging among others. More task types are rolling out in the coming days including WiFi-only, SIM-only and S&R public release. 

Saying “we’ve experienced nothing but app updates” while ignoring what’s been released today and what’s about to land isn’t fair to the team that’s been working around the clock to deliver it. 

Dismissing real progress because it hasn’t hit peak volume yet doesn’t reflect what’s actually happening on the ground.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
Volume scaling in telecoms doesn’t happen on a community member’s timeline. It happens when customers complete validation, sign off on quality, and begin routing production traffic. 

That process has been happening. Telling you contracts are signed and customers are satisfied isn’t a deflection. 

It’s the step that comes before volume. You don’t get one without the other.

As for what to tell your ULOs. Tell them what was announced today yesterday. SMS testing live. Web portals live. New task types on the way. Scout and Runner approaching public release with meaningful new functionality for the network. 

CLI has been live for two months. In telecoms terms that’s nothing. The validation period is what builds the foundation for serious, sustained traffic. 

That traffic is designed to grow and network activity should reflect that over time. 

But if you’re expecting a network that’s building the largest edge infrastructure of its kind to hit full capacity in eight weeks, the expectation needs adjusting. Not the delivery.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
CLI testing has had tests running throughout the validation period. The fact that not every ULO has received calls doesn’t mean no calls are happening on the network - it means customers have been selectively testing across specific routes, regions and devices as part of their validation process. That’s standard for any commercial telecoms service before scaling to full production volume. Volume will arrive.

As for SMS - it launched yesterday and is a different product serving a different customer need. Content verification and sender ID testing is in high demand across the industry. We’re not going to guarantee you a specific volumes on day one because that depends on customer usage, but the commercial interest is there and active, and decent volumes will arrive to the network. 

How you choose to communicate with your ULOs is your decision. What we can tell you is that tasks are live, have commercial demand behind them, and volume across both is expected to grow and additional tasks are on the way. S&R has incredible reward potential, as an example.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
Different countries reward different UPs per minute - so the rate isn’t fixed globally, it varies depending on the destination and route being tested. The reason SIM costs aren’t reimbursed for Runners is because the reward potential significantly outweighs the cost of a plan. During Alpha testing, some participants were seeing returns that made the SIM cost negligible by comparison.

For lower income countries - the cost of SIM plans is also significantly lower in those regions, so the barrier to entry is proportionally smaller. The economics are designed to work across all markets.
